note: Tier thresholds derived from lab captures (2026-06). Tune from analyze_master2.py corpus runs. Ship as data, not code — tune without an app update.

tiers.name: Robust
tiers.description: 560–930 bps. Survives mediocre setups and AAC re-encode. The baseline tape tier.
tiers.advice_pass: Your setup qualifies for Robust tier. You should be able to read the tape reliably.
tiers.advice_low_snr: Move the phone closer to the speaker, or reduce ambient noise.
tiers.advice_low_snr_p10: Some frequency bands are weak. Reduce room reflections or try a different phone position.
tiers.advice_flutter: Deck flutter is high. Service or replace the tape deck.
tiers.advice_noise: Room noise is too loud. Find a quieter environment.

tiers.name: Turbo
tiers.description: 1.5–2.5 kbps. Requires a decent setup and lossless capture. No AAC.
tiers.advice_pass: Excellent! Your setup qualifies for Turbo tier.
tiers.advice_low_snr: SNR is a bit low for Turbo. Move the phone closer or into a quieter room.
tiers.advice_low_snr_p10: Worst-case carrier SNR is marginal. Try reducing room reflections.
tiers.advice_flutter: Deck flutter exceeds Turbo requirements. The deck needs servicing.
tiers.advice_noise: Ambient noise is too high for Turbo tier. Try a quieter room.

tiers.name: Moonshot
tiers.description: 4–5 kbps. Best setups only: quiet room, phone close, lossless capture, low flutter.
tiers.advice_pass: Outstanding setup! You qualify for the Moonshot tier.
tiers.advice_low_snr: SNR needs to be higher for Moonshot. Phone right next to the speaker, quiet room.
tiers.advice_low_snr_p10: Worst-case SNR is the gating factor. Eliminate reflections (soft furnishings help).
tiers.advice_flutter: Deck flutter is the limiting factor for Moonshot. Consider a well-maintained or freshly-serviced deck.
tiers.advice_noise: Noise floor is too high for Moonshot. Record at night or in a very quiet room.
